Largest contributor: debt_consolidation ($+12.2M)
In [164]:
# ── Waterfall chart by purpose ─────────────────────────────────────
# Show top purposes by delta NI, group the rest as "Other"
TOP_N = 5
purpose_sim_filtered = purpose_sim[purpose_sim["ead_pre"] >= 1e4].copy()

top = purpose_sim_filtered.head(TOP_N).copy()
other_delta = purpose_sim_filtered.iloc[TOP_N:]["delta_net_income_M"].sum()
other_ni_pre = purpose_sim_filtered.iloc[TOP_N:]["net_income_pre"].sum() / 1e6

total_ni_pre = purpose_sim_filtered["net_income_pre"].sum() / 1e6
total_ni_post = purpose_sim_filtered["net_income_post"].sum() / 1e6

# Build waterfall components
labels = (
    ["Current\nNet Income"]
    + [f"↑ {r['purpose'].replace('_', ' ').title()}" for _, r in top.iterrows()]
    + ["Other\nPurposes", "Post-Strategy\nNet Income"]
)
values = [total_ni_pre] + list(top["delta_net_income_M"]) + [other_delta, total_ni_post]
colors = (
    [ORANGE]
    + [GREEN if v >= 0 else RED for v in top["delta_net_income_M"]]
    + [GRAY, GREEN]
)

# Compute bottoms
bottoms = [0]
running = total_ni_pre
for v in values[1:-1]:
    bottoms.append(running)
    running += v
bottoms.append(0)

# Plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

for i, (label, val, color, bottom) in enumerate(zip(labels, values, colors, bottoms)):
    ax.bar(i, val, bottom=bottom, color=color, edgecolor="white", width=0.5, alpha=0.9)
    if i == 0 or i == len(labels) - 1:
        ypos = bottom + val / 2
        ax.text(
            i,
            ypos,
            f"${val:.1f}M",
            ha="center",
            va="center",
            fontsize=10,
            fontweight="bold",
            color="white",
        )
    else:
        ypos = bottom + val + (0.5 if val >= 0 else -0.5)
        ax.text(
            i,
            ypos,
            f"${val:+.1f}M",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom" if val >= 0 else "top",
            fontsize=9,
            fontweight="bold",
        )

# Connector lines
bar_tops = [b + v for b, v in zip(bottoms, values)]
for i in range(len(values) - 1):
    ax.plot(
        [i + 0.25, i + 0.75],
        [bar_tops[i], bar_tops[i]],
        color="gray",
        linewidth=0.8,
        linestyle="--",
    )

ax.set_xticks(range(len(labels)))
ax.set_xticklabels(labels, fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylabel("Net Income ($M)")
ax.set_title(
    "Net Income Bridge by Loan Purpose — Current vs Post-Strategy", fontweight="bold"
)
ax.set_ylim(0, max(total_ni_pre, total_ni_post) * 1.15)
Out[164]:
(0.0, 426.26464760241174)

3.6 Validation: Pricing Framework Internal Consistency¶

Five checks. No future default data needed.

Backtest scope note: The 2016-vintage backtest provides supporting evidence that the PD-LGD calibration is reasonable, but it does not fully validate the 2018Q4 portfolio’s future performance.

Check 2 note: Validation benchmark is the mean realized loss across 4 quarters of 2016 data, not total. Total realized loss = EL + UL; pricing only targets EL. Pooling across quarters helps reduce idiosyncratic noise.

In [165]:
print("=" * 65)
print("VALIDATION CHECKS")
print("=" * 65)

# Check 1: Gap directionality (centralized here)
grade_gaps = (
    df.groupby("grade")
    .apply(lambda x: np.average(x["pricing_gap"], weights=x["ead"]))
    .reindex(["A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G"])
)
is_monotone = all(
    grade_gaps.iloc[i] >= grade_gaps.iloc[i + 1] for i in range(len(grade_gaps) - 1)
)
print(f" Check 1 — Gap Directionality (A→G monotonically decreasing):")
print(f"  Result: {is_monotone}")
for g, v in grade_gaps.items():
    print(f"  Grade {g}: {v:+.2%}")

# Check 2: EL parameter calibration vs mean realized loss (2016 cohort)
print(f" Check 2 — EL Parameter Calibration (vs mean realized loss, 2016 cohort):")
print(f"  Correct benchmark : MEAN realized loss (pooled 4Q 2016)")
print(f"  NOT total loss    : total = EL + UL; pricing only targets EL")
print(f"  Pooling across quarters helps reduce idiosyncratic noise")
print(f"  2016 cohort 12m pooled (from loss_02 backtest):")
print(f"    Model EL (PD×LGD×EAD)  : $339.98M")
print(f"    Mean realized loss      : $362.84M")
print(f"    EL coverage ratio       : 93.7%  (> 90% = well-calibrated)")
print(
    f"  → Supports calibration, but does not fully validate 2018Q4 forward performance"
)

# Check 3: RAROC distribution sensibility
raroc_by_grade = (
    df.groupby("grade")
    .apply(lambda x: x["net_income"].sum() / x["ec"].sum())
    .reindex(["A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G"])
)
print(f" Check 3 — RAROC Distribution Sensibility:")
print(f"  (Diagnostic only; RAROC does not assign buckets)")
for g, v in raroc_by_grade.items():
    status = "above hurdle" if v >= HURDLE_RATE else "below hurdle"
    print(f"  Grade {g}: RAROC = {v:.1%} ({status})")

# Check 4: PD model credibility
print(f" Check 4 — PD Model Credibility:")
print(f"  PD column detected     : {PD_COL}")
print(f"  PD aggregation method  : {PD_AGG_METHOD}")
print(f"  2016-vintage 12m backtest coverage: 0.937 (from loss_02)")
print(f"  → Supporting evidence for applying PD calibration to 2018Q4")

# Check 5: Statistical significance of bucket assignments
n_reliable = df["ci_reliable"].sum()
n_above_cap = df["above_pricing_cap"].sum()
n_sig_reprice_before_override = df["sig_reprice"].sum()
n_assigned_reprice = (df["bucket"] == "Reprice").sum()
n_sig_reprice_declined = (df["sig_reprice"] & df["above_pricing_cap"]).sum()
n_sig_grow = df["sig_grow"].sum()
n_hold = (df["bucket"] == "Hold / Monitor").sum()
n_decline = (df["bucket"] == "Decline / Do not originate").sum()
n_grow = (df["bucket"] == "Grow").sum()

print(f" Check 5 — Statistical Significance of Bucket Assignments ({CI_LEVEL:.0%} CI):")
print(f"  Segments with reliable CI (n >= {MIN_N}) : {n_reliable} / {len(df)}")
print(f"  Above pricing cap (auto-Decline)         : {n_above_cap}")
print(
    f"  Significantly underpriced before cap override : {n_sig_reprice_before_override}"
)
print(f"  Reprice assigned after cap override           : {n_assigned_reprice}")
print(f"  Underpriced but above cap → Decline           : {n_sig_reprice_declined}")
print(f"  Significantly overpriced (signal)             : {n_sig_grow}")
print(f"  Hold / Monitor assigned                       : {n_hold}")
print(
    f"  Bucket counts (Grow/Reprice/Decline/Hold): {n_grow}/{n_assigned_reprice}/{n_decline}/{n_hold}"
)
print(
    f"  Bucket total check                        : {n_grow + n_assigned_reprice + n_decline + n_hold} / {len(df)}"
)
print(
    f"  → Buckets are assigned by priority + approximate CI significance; RAROC is separate"
)

portfolio_raroc = df["net_income"].sum() / df["ec"].sum()
print(f" Check 6 — Portfolio RAROC vs Hurdle:")
print(f"  Portfolio RAROC            : {portfolio_raroc:.1%}")
print(f"  Hurdle rate                : {HURDLE_RATE:.1%}")
print(
    f"  Gap to hurdle              : {(portfolio_raroc - HURDLE_RATE)*100:+.1f} percentage points"
)

# Check 7: RAROC by purpose — cross-purpose sanity check
print(f"\nCheck 7 — RAROC Distribution by Purpose:")
print(f"  (Diagnostic only; confirms RAROC pattern is consistent across purposes)")

raroc_by_purpose = (
    df.groupby("purpose")
    .apply(lambda x: x["net_income"].sum() / x["ec"].sum())
    .sort_values()
)
n_above = (raroc_by_purpose >= HURDLE_RATE).sum()
n_below = (raroc_by_purpose < HURDLE_RATE).sum()
for purpose, v in raroc_by_purpose.items():
    status = "above hurdle" if v >= HURDLE_RATE else "BELOW hurdle ⚠"
    print(f"  {purpose:<22}: RAROC = {v:.1%} ({status})")
print(f"  → {n_above}/{len(raroc_by_purpose)} purposes above hurdle rate")
print(
    f"  → Small Business is the only below-hurdle purpose, "
    f"consistent with pricing gap analysis"
)

# Check 8: Reprice NII concentration check
print(f"\nCheck 8 — Reprice NII Concentration:")
print(f"  (Checks whether repricing opportunity is concentrated in one purpose)")

reprice_by_purpose = (
    df[df["bucket"] == "Reprice"]
    .groupby("purpose")
    .apply(
        lambda x: pd.Series(
            {
                "ead_M": x["ead"].sum() / 1e6,
                "incremental_NII_M": ((x["r_req"] - x["avg_intrate"]) * x["ead"]).sum()
                / 1e6,
            }
        )
    )
    .sort_values("incremental_NII_M", ascending=False)
    .reset_index()
)

total_nii = reprice_by_purpose["incremental_NII_M"].sum()
print(f"  Total incremental NII from repricing : ${total_nii:.1f}M")
for _, row in reprice_by_purpose.iterrows():
    share = row["incremental_NII_M"] / total_nii * 100
    print(
        f"  {row['purpose']:<22}: "
        f"EAD ${row['ead_M']:.1f}M | "
        f"NII ${row['incremental_NII_M']:.1f}M ({share:.0f}%)"
    )

top_share = reprice_by_purpose.iloc[0]["incremental_NII_M"] / total_nii * 100
top_purpose = reprice_by_purpose.iloc[0]["purpose"]
print(
    f"  → Top purpose ({top_purpose}) accounts for "
    f"{top_share:.0f}% of total repricing NII"
)
if top_share > 50:
    print(
        f"  → Concentration is high: repricing impact depends heavily "
        f"on one purpose — execution risk should be noted"
    )

print(f"{'='*65}")
print(f"All six checks completed for the 2018Q4 active portfolio.")
print(f"(PD: {PD_COL}, agg: {PD_AGG_METHOD})")

=================================================================
VALIDATION CHECKS
=================================================================
 Check 1 — Gap Directionality (A→G monotonically decreasing):
  Result: False
  Grade A: -1.18%
  Grade B: +1.37%
  Grade C: +3.33%
  Grade D: +4.86%
  Grade E: +5.25%
  Grade F: +4.17%
  Grade G: +1.82%
 Check 2 — EL Parameter Calibration (vs mean realized loss, 2016 cohort):
  Correct benchmark : MEAN realized loss (pooled 4Q 2016)
  NOT total loss    : total = EL + UL; pricing only targets EL
  Pooling across quarters helps reduce idiosyncratic noise
  2016 cohort 12m pooled (from loss_02 backtest):
    Model EL (PD×LGD×EAD)  : $339.98M
    Mean realized loss      : $362.84M
    EL coverage ratio       : 93.7%  (> 90% = well-calibrated)
  → Supports calibration, but does not fully validate 2018Q4 forward performance
 Check 3 — RAROC Distribution Sensibility:
  (Diagnostic only; RAROC does not assign buckets)
  Grade A: RAROC = 3.5% (below hurdle)
  Grade B: RAROC = 29.1% (above hurdle)
  Grade C: RAROC = 45.2% (above hurdle)
  Grade D: RAROC = 54.1% (above hurdle)
  Grade E: RAROC = 50.7% (above hurdle)
  Grade F: RAROC = 37.3% (above hurdle)
  Grade G: RAROC = 20.4% (above hurdle)
 Check 4 — PD Model Credibility:
  PD column detected     : pd_12m_logit
  PD aggregation method  : EAD-weighted (loan_amnt)
  2016-vintage 12m backtest coverage: 0.937 (from loss_02)
  → Supporting evidence for applying PD calibration to 2018Q4
 Check 5 — Statistical Significance of Bucket Assignments (95% CI):
  Segments with reliable CI (n >= 30) : 141 / 170
  Above pricing cap (auto-Decline)         : 10
  Significantly underpriced before cap override : 24
  Reprice assigned after cap override           : 19
  Underpriced but above cap → Decline           : 5
  Significantly overpriced (signal)             : 93
  Hold / Monitor assigned                       : 48
  Bucket counts (Grow/Reprice/Decline/Hold): 93/19/10/48
  Bucket total check                        : 170 / 170
  → Buckets are assigned by priority + approximate CI significance; RAROC is separate
 Check 6 — Portfolio RAROC vs Hurdle:
  Portfolio RAROC            : 34.8%
  Hurdle rate                : 12.0%
  Gap to hurdle              : +22.8 percentage points

Check 7 — RAROC Distribution by Purpose:
  (Diagnostic only; confirms RAROC pattern is consistent across purposes)
  small_business        : RAROC = 9.9% (BELOW hurdle ⚠)
  moving                : RAROC = 13.8% (above hurdle)
  renewable_energy      : RAROC = 17.3% (above hurdle)
  medical               : RAROC = 21.5% (above hurdle)
  vacation              : RAROC = 24.2% (above hurdle)
  house                 : RAROC = 29.1% (above hurdle)
  other                 : RAROC = 31.5% (above hurdle)
  major_purchase        : RAROC = 34.0% (above hurdle)
  car                   : RAROC = 34.7% (above hurdle)
  credit_card           : RAROC = 35.4% (above hurdle)
  debt_consolidation    : RAROC = 35.5% (above hurdle)
  home_improvement      : RAROC = 36.5% (above hurdle)
  wedding               : RAROC = 38.1% (above hurdle)
  educational           : RAROC = 77.9% (above hurdle)
  → 13/14 purposes above hurdle rate
  → Small Business is the only below-hurdle purpose, consistent with pricing gap analysis

Check 8 — Reprice NII Concentration:
  (Checks whether repricing opportunity is concentrated in one purpose)
  Total incremental NII from repricing : $23.8M
  debt_consolidation    : EAD $838.6M | NII $12.1M (51%)
  credit_card           : EAD $554.3M | NII $6.5M (27%)
  home_improvement      : EAD $122.4M | NII $1.6M (7%)
  other                 : EAD $71.2M | NII $1.1M (5%)
  small_business        : EAD $60.0M | NII $1.0M (4%)
  major_purchase        : EAD $33.5M | NII $0.5M (2%)
  medical               : EAD $24.8M | NII $0.3M (1%)
  house                 : EAD $15.4M | NII $0.3M (1%)
  moving                : EAD $10.7M | NII $0.2M (1%)
  car                   : EAD $11.5M | NII $0.1M (1%)
  vacation              : EAD $4.9M | NII $0.1M (0%)
  renewable_energy      : EAD $0.7M | NII $0.0M (0%)
  → Top purpose (debt_consolidation) accounts for 51% of total repricing NII
  → Concentration is high: repricing impact depends heavily on one purpose — execution risk should be noted
=================================================================
All six checks completed for the 2018Q4 active portfolio.
(PD: pd_12m_logit, agg: EAD-weighted (loan_amnt))

Validation: Pricing Framework Internal Consistency¶

Six internal consistency checks were conducted on the 2018Q4 active portfolio. No future default data is required — all checks use available model outputs and 2016 backtest data.

Check 1 — Gap Directionality (Result: False) The pricing gap does not decrease monotonically from Grade A to G. Grade A remains a key under-compensated area (notably A-36m), while multiple mid-grade segments show positive pricing gaps. This non-monotonic pattern is economically informative rather than a framework failure.

Check 2 — EL Parameter Calibration (Pass) The model EL (PD × LGD × EAD) of $339.98M covers 93.7% of the mean realized loss of $362.84M from the pooled 2016 cohort (4Q average). Pooling across quarters helps reduce idiosyncratic noise. This supports, but does not fully validate, the 2018Q4 portfolio’s forward performance.

Check 3 — RAROC Distribution Sensibility (Diagnostic) RAROC is evaluated using merged L4 economic capital (K_pct) and interpreted as a capital-efficiency diagnostic, not a bucket assignment rule. Results indicate uneven segment profitability, with weak RAROC in selected Grade A segments and strong capital-adjusted returns in several C–E 60m segments.

Check 4 — PD Model Credibility (Pass) The PD model (pd_12m_logit, EAD-weighted) achieved a 2016-vintage 12-month backtest coverage of 93.7%, providing supporting calibration evidence.

Check 5 — Statistical Significance of Bucket Assignments (Pass) Bucket assignment follows priority rules: pricing-cap override first, then approximate 95% CI significance for Reprice/Grow, else Hold. The check separates: (1) significantly underpriced before override, (2) final Reprice assignments, (3) underpriced but forced to Decline due to pricing feasibility. This prevents double counting and keeps bucket totals internally consistent.

Check 6 — Portfolio RAROC vs Hurdle (Pass) Portfolio RAROC remains above hurdle and is reported with the correct unit as percentage points (not basis points) when presenting the hurdle gap.

Check 7 — RAROC Distribution by Purpose (Diagnostic) 13 out of 14 purposes exceed the 12% hurdle rate, confirming broad capital-adjusted profitability. Small Business (9.9%) is the only exception, consistent with its under-pricing identified in §3.3.

Check 8 — Reprice NII Concentration (Pass with caveat) Total repricing NII is $23.8M, but heavily concentrated: Debt Consolidation (51%, $12.1M) and Credit Card (27%, $6.5M) together account for 78% of the total. Since all Reprice segments are Grade A 36-month loans, execution risk is elevated — high-quality borrowers may resist rate increases. A phased approach is advisable before rolling out to the $838.6M Debt Consolidation segment.

Overall Assessment The framework supports a targeted risk management strategy rather than blanket tightening: reprice under-compensated segments, decline infeasible above-cap segments, and selectively grow statistically over-priced segments while using RAROC to validate capital-adjusted profitability.

3.7 Save L2 Outputs (for L3 / L4)¶

In [166]:
l2_output = {
    "pd_column_detected": PD_COL,
    "pd_aggregation_method": PD_AGG_METHOD,
    "lgd_const": LGD_CONST,
    "funding_cost": FUNDING_COST,
    "op_cost": OP_COST,
    "target_margin": TARGET_MARGIN,
    "hurdle_rate": HURDLE_RATE,
    "max_pricing_rate": MAX_PRICING_RATE,
    "ci_level": CI_LEVEL,
    "min_n_for_ci": MIN_N,
    "ci_method_note": "Approximate CI from within-segment interest-rate dispersion; excludes full PD model uncertainty.",
    "active_ead_$B": float(df["ead"].sum() / 1e9),
    "portfolio_avg_r_lc": float(
        (df["avg_intrate"] * df["ead"]).sum() / df["ead"].sum()
    ),
    "portfolio_avg_r_req": float((df["r_req"] * df["ead"]).sum() / df["ead"].sum()),
    "portfolio_avg_gap": float((df["pricing_gap"] * df["ead"]).sum() / df["ead"].sum()),
    "portfolio_raroc_current": float(df["net_income"].sum() / df["ec"].sum()),
    "portfolio_raroc_post": float(sim["net_income_post"].sum() / sim["ec_post"].sum()),
    "net_income_current_$M": float(df["net_income"].sum() / 1e6),
    "net_income_post_$M": float(sim["net_income_post"].sum() / 1e6),
    "el_current_$M": float(df["el_12m_model"].sum() / 1e6),
    "el_post_$M": float(sim["el_post"].sum() / 1e6),
    "segments_reprice": int((df["bucket"] == "Reprice").sum()),
    "segments_decline": int((df["bucket"] == "Decline / Do not originate").sum()),
    "segments_grow": int((df["bucket"] == "Grow").sum()),
    "segments_hold": int((df["bucket"] == "Hold / Monitor").sum()),
    "ec_source": "L4 K_pct by grade",
    "ec_total_$B": float(df["ec"].sum() / 1e9),
    "raroc_by_purpose": {
        purpose: float(raroc) for purpose, raroc in raroc_by_purpose.items()
    },
    "reprice_nii_by_purpose_$M": {
        row["purpose"]: float(row["incremental_NII_M"])
        for _, row in reprice_by_purpose.iterrows()
    },
    "n_purposes_above_hurdle": int(n_above),
    "n_purposes_below_hurdle": int(n_below),
}

with open(OUT_DIR / "l2_pricing.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(l2_output, f, indent=2)

df[
    [
        "grade",
        "term_num",
        "purpose",
        "n",
        "ead",
        "avg_pd",
        "avg_intrate",
        "std_intrate",
        "n_intrate",
        "el_premium",
        "el_12m_model",
        "el_12m_l1",
        "r_req_raw",
        "above_pricing_cap",
        "r_req",
        "pricing_gap",
        "approx_gap_ci_low",
        "approx_gap_ci_high",
        "ci_reliable",
        "sig_reprice",
        "sig_grow",
        "bucket",
        "ec",
        "raroc",
    ]
].to_csv(OUT_DIR / "l2_segment_table.csv", index=False)

action_table_purpose.to_csv(OUT_DIR / "l2_action_table_by_purpose.csv", index=False)

print(f"Saved: l2_pricing.json")
print(f"Saved: l2_segment_table.csv  (includes approximate CI and significance flags)")
print(f"Saved: l2_action_table.csv")
print(f"Saved: l2_action_table_by_purpose.csv")

print()
print("--- L2 SUMMARY ---")
for k, v in l2_output.items():
    if isinstance(v, dict):
        print(f"  {k}:")
        for sub_k, sub_v in v.items():
            if isinstance(sub_v, float):
                print(
                    f"    {sub_k:<22} {sub_v:.2%}"
                    if any(kw in k for kw in ["raroc", "rate", "gap"])
                    else f"    {sub_k:<22} {sub_v:.2f}"
                )
            else:
                print(f"    {sub_k:<22} {sub_v}")
    elif isinstance(v, float):
        if any(
            kw in k for kw in ["rate", "raroc", "gap", "margin", "cost", "ci_level"]
        ):
            print(f"  {k:<38} {v:.2%}")
        else:
            print(f"  {k:<38} {v:.2f}")
    else:
        print(f"  {k:<38} {v}")

Saved: l2_pricing.json
Saved: l2_segment_table.csv  (includes approximate CI and significance flags)
Saved: l2_action_table.csv
Saved: l2_action_table_by_purpose.csv
